questions.question: Which among the following constitute central nervous system?
questions.explanation: The brain and spinal cord constitute the central nervous system. They receive information from all parts of the body and integrate it.

questions.question: Involuntary actions are controlled by which part of brain?
questions.explanation: All involuntary actions including blood pressure, salivation and vomiting are controlled by the medulla in the hind-brain.

questions.question: Gustatory receptors detect which among the following senses?
questions.explanation: Receptors are usually located in our sense organs, such as the inner ear, the nose, the tongue etc. Gustatory receptors will detect taste while olfactory receptors will detect smell.

questions.question: In which among the following type of reproduction, a new individual organism is formed from the body parts matured organism?
questions.explanation: Many fully differentiated organisms have the ability to give rise to new individual organisms from their body parts. For example, simple animals like Hydra and Planaria can be cut into any number of pieces and each piece grows into a complete organism. This is known as regeneration.

questions.question: DNA can be found in which among the following organelles of a cell?
questions.explanation: All these organelles have their own DNA and ribosomes- protein synthesizing organelle. While nucleus and ribosomes are present in both animal and plant cell, Plastids are present only in plant cells.

questions.question: Leukemia is caused by abnormal production of _________?
questions.explanation: In leukemia, abnormal blood cells are produced in the bone marrow. Usually, leukemia involves the production of abnormal white blood cells -- the cells responsible for fighting infection. However, the abnormal cells in leukemia do not function in the same way as normal white blood cells.

questions.question: Which is the filtration unit of kidneys?
questions.explanation: Each capillary cluster in the kidney is associated with the cup-shaped end of a tube that collects the filtered urine. Each kidney has large numbers of these filtration units called nephrons packed close together.

questions.question: Shape and size of cells are related to __________?
questions.explanation: The shape and size of cells are related to the specific function they perform.

questions.question: ATP- form of energy stands for _________?
questions.explanation: Different molecules move in and out of the cell through a type of transport requiring use of energy in the form of ATP. Adenosine triphosphate (ATP) is considered by biologists to be the energy currency of life.

questions.question: Which among the following pigment is called as respiratory pigment?
questions.explanation: In human beings, the respiratory pigment is haemoglobin which has a very high affinity for oxygen. This pigment is present in the red blood corpuscles.

questions.question: The distance between the pole and the principal focus of a spherical mirror is called as _____?
questions.explanation: The distance between the pole and the principal focus of a spherical mirror is called the focal length. It is represented by the letter f.

questions.question: Refraction of light is due to change in which among the following?
questions.explanation: Refraction is due to change in the speed of light as it enters from one transparent medium to another.

questions.question: The SI Unit of electric current is _______?
questions.explanation: The electric current is expressed by a unit called ampere (A), named after the French scientist, Andre-Marie Ampere (1775 - 1836). One ampere is constituted by the flow of one coulomb of charge per second, that is, 1 A = 1 C/1 s.

questions.question: Which among the following gases is filled in an electric bulb?
questions.explanation: The electric bulbs are usually filled with chemically inactive nitrogen and argon gases to prolong the life of filament.

questions.question: Which among the following converts kinetic energy into electric energy?
questions.explanation: A wind turbine is a machine for converting the kinetic energy in wind into electrical energy. Moving air (wind) is used to rotate a machine called a turbine. Inside the wind turbine is an electrical generator. It's the electrical generator that transforms the kinetic energy into electrical energy.

questions.question: Photovoltaic cells are predominantly made of ________?
questions.explanation: Photovoltaic cells are solar cells. A photovoltaic cell (PV cell) is a specialized semiconductor diode that converts visible light into direct current (DC). It is made up of silicon.

questions.question: Which part of eye regulates and controls the amount of light?
questions.explanation: Iris is a dark muscular diaphragm that controls the size of the pupil. The pupil regulates and controls the amount of light.

questions.question: Acceleration due to earth's gravitational force (g) at north pole will be ________?
questions.explanation: The earth is not a perfect sphere. As the radius of the earth increases from the poles to the equator, the value of g becomes greater at the poles than at the equator.

questions.question: When a hollow block and a solid block of rock of same dimensions are dropped from same height, which among the following can be observed?
options: Hollow block reaches the ground first
Solid block reaches the ground first
Both block reach at same time
Mass of the blocks determine which to reach ground first
Velocity achieved by the blocks determine which to reach ground first
questions.explanation: The acceleration experienced by an object is independent of its mass. This means that all objects hollow or solid, big or small, fall at the same rate. So, both blocks reach at the same time.

questions.question: Power of which among the following lens is positive?
questions.explanation: Power of a convex lens is positive and that of a concave lens is negative.

questions.question: Which among the following acid is produced by string of bees?
questions.explanation: Bee-sting leaves an acid which causes pain and irritation. Bee venom contains methanoic acid (also known as formic acid)

questions.question: Which is referred to as Milk of magnesia used as an antacid?
questions.explanation: During indigestion the stomach produces too much acid and this causes pain and irritation. Antacids neutralise the excess acid. Magnesium hydroxide (Milk of magnesia), a mild base is used as an antacid.

questions.question: Which among the following is an ore of Copper?
questions.explanation: Chalcopyrite is composed of Copper iron sulphide. It is a primary source of ore for copper.

questions.question: There are totally how many periods in modern periodic table?
questions.explanation: The Modern Periodic Table has 18 vertical columns known as 'groups' and 7 horizontal rows known as 'periods'.

questions.question: Who discovered neutron?
questions.explanation: In 1932, J. Chadwick discovered another subatomic particle which had no charge and a mass nearly equal to that of a proton. It was eventually named as neutron.

questions.question: Which among the following changes in an isotope?
questions.explanation: Isotopes are defined as the atoms of the same element, having the same atomic number but different mass numbers.

questions.question: Oxalic acid is naturally found in ________?
questions.explanation: Oxalic acid is naturally found in tomatoes. As tomatoes ripens, the amount of Vitamin C decreases and the amount of oxalic acid increases.

questions.question: Which element is used for radiation protection?
questions.explanation: Lead shielding helps protect from radiation because of its high molecular density. Effective at stopping gamma rays and x-rays, lead is used as radiation protection in applications ranging from x-ray imaging and PET rooms to nuclear reactors.

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT True about tooth decay?
options: pH of mouth will be lower than 5.5
sugars in the mouth are degraded to erode the tooth enamel
Bacteria play a major role in decay
questions.explanation: All the above-mentioned statements about tooth decay are true.

questions.question: Carbon dioxide produced during reaction between an acid and hydrogen carbonate of which among the following chemical element results in proofing of bread?
questions.explanation: While making baking powder, which is a mixture of baking soda (sodium hydrogen carbonate) and a mild edible acid, carbon dioxide is released. Carbon dioxide produced during the reaction causes bread or cake to rise making them soft and spongy, which is proofing of bread.
